questions.question: What did Paul do when Festus asked if he was willing to go to Jerusalem for trial?
Which king came to Caesarea to visit Festus and heard Paul's case?
Festus found Paul guilty of crimes against Roman law.
Paul said, 'I am standing before _____'s judgment seat, where I ought to be tried.'
Festus said to Agrippa, 'It seems _____ to me to send a prisoner without specifying the charges against him.'
